title: hieracium murorum subsp. oblongum
intro: Hieracium murorum subsp. oblongum, also known as Hawkweed, is a plant species within the dandelion family, characterized by its small, daisy-like flowers and bitter taste. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly as a digestive aid and to treat liver and kidney issues due to its liver-protective and anti-inflammatory compounds. The plant has been found to have various health benefits, including reducing inflammation, improving liver function, and acting as an antioxidant. Hieracium murorum subsp. oblongum can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, with some herbalists also using it in salves and creams for topical application.
